revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:荒川 (山梨県)]]
{{Infobox river | name = Arakawa (Yamanashi) | image = [[File:The Arakawa river and the Kofu city area.JPG|300px]] | image_caption = Arakawa River and Kofu City viewed from Chishu Bridge (May 2013) | river_system = {{ill|Fuji River|en|Fuji River|qid=Q596110|lt=Fuji River|1=Fuji River}} | basin_countries = Japan | length = | source1 = [[Mount Kokushi|Kokushigatake]] | source1_location = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}} | mouth = {{ill|Fuefuki River|en|Fuefuki River|qid=Q11599751|lt=Fuefuki River|1=Fuefuki River}} | mouth_location = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}} | mouth_elevation = | basin_size = | subdivisions = Yamanashi Prefecture ([[:en:Kofu|Kofu]], [[Kai]]) }} '''Arakawa''' (荒川) is a [[first-class river]] in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, part of the {{ill|Fuji River|en|Fuji River|qid=Q596110|lt=Fuji River|1=Fuji River}} system. It flows mainly through [[:en:Kofu|Kofu]]. == Overview == Arakawa originates in the [[:en:Kanto Mountains|Kanto Mountains]], near [[Mount Kokushi|Kokushigatake]], in northwestern [[Yamanashi City]]. Flowing southward, it forms the boundary between Yamanashi and Kofu before entering the city. The upper reaches feature scenic spots like {{ill|Shosenkyo|ja|昇仙峡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shosenkyo|1=Shōsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9336171}} and religious sites such as {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}}. Within the [[Kōfu Basin|Kofu Basin]], the river turns southwest, briefly flowing into [[Kai City]], before turning southeast to re-enter Kofu and merge with {{ill|Aikawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|相川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa River (Yamanashi)|1=Aikawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q123029762}} near Arakawa Bridge. It continues southward, paralleling national roads, eventually joining the {{ill|Fuefuki River|en|Fuefuki River|qid=Q11599751|lt=Fuefuki River|1=Fuefuki River}} near major highways like the [[:en:Chuo Expressway|Chuo Expressway]]. == History == === The Name Arakawa and "Wasuregawa" === According to the historical ''{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11577871}}'' (1814), the name "Arakawa" likely derives from the Japanese word for "violent" (荒), reflecting the river's frequent flooding. The text also notes references to a "Wasuregawa" (忘川) in classical poetry, such as those by {{ill|Mibu no Tadamine|en|Mibu no Tadamine|qid=Q1886933|lt=Mibu no Tadamine|1=Mibu no Tadamine}}, where it served as a poetic toponym. However, no river named "Wasuregawa" exists in Kai Province, leading to debates about which river it represents. Edo-period scholars such as {{ill|Ogyū Sorai|en|Ogyū Sorai|qid=Q369158|lt=Ogyu Sorai|1=Ogyū Sorai}} suggested that "Wasuregawa" might refer to "Arakawa," noting the similarity between their names in cursive script. === Arakawa in the Medieval Period === The area around the Arakawa basin, including [[Kai City]]'s Shima Kamijo district, was historically known as "Iida Riverbank." It was the site of notable battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Iida Riverbank|ja|飯田河原の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iida Riverbank|qq=}} in 1521, during which {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|en|Takeda Nobutora|qid=Q1192628|lt=Takeda Nobutora|1=Takeda Nobutora}}'s forces defeated the invading troops of {{ill|Imagawa clan|en|Imagawa clan|qid=Q1764434|lt=Imagawa clan|1=Imagawa clan}}. === River Fishing === During the Edo period, Arakawa was renowned for its {{ill|Ayu|en|Ayu|qid=Q361476|lt=Ayu|1=Ayu}} (sweetfish). Documents like ''{{ill|Urami Kanwa|ja|裏見寒話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urami Kanwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1752) list Arakawa alongside Fuefuki and Kamanashi Rivers as prominent fishing locations. Historical depictions of fishing, such as in the late-Edo ''Kai Meisho Jukoroku,'' also illustrate cormorant fishing practices. == Flood Control == Like other rivers in the Fuji River system, Arakawa frequently caused flooding during heavy rains, devastating the urban areas of Kofu. Following the catastrophic [[Ise Bay Typhoon]] in 1959, the construction of flood control measures, including the {{ill|Arakawa Dam (Yamanashi, Japan)|en|Arakawa Dam (Yamanashi, Japan)|qid=Q11618508|lt=Arakawa Dam (Yamanashi)|1=Arakawa Dam (Yamanashi)}} in 1986, was prioritized. The river has been designated for flood forecasting since 2008. <gallery> Shosenkyo Arakawa-1.jpg|Arakawa and Shosenkyo Museum of Shadowgraph (2018) Shosenkyo Arakawa-1a.jpg|Arakawa and Sengataki Falls (2018) Shosenkyo Arakawa-1b.jpg|Arakawa and Shosenkyo Bridge (2018) </gallery> == Municipalities in the Basin == * [[Kofu City]] * [[Kai City]] == Major Tributaries == * Kamezawa River * Obina River (including {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11405318}}) * {{ill|Aikawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|相川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa River (Yamanashi)|1=Aikawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q123029762}} (featuring {{ill|Ryugaike Pond|ja|竜ヶ池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryugaike Pond|1=Ryugaike Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30936577}}) * Mikumigawa River == Major Bridges == * Sensho Bridge * Otowa Bridge * Fujimi Bridge * Name TBD (Chuo Main Line) * Nagamatsujibashi Bridge * New Arakawa Bridge * Iito Bridge * Chishu Bridge * Saihi Bridge (Kofu Bypass) * Manzai Bridge * Various crossings of the [[:en:Chuo Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] and [[Shin-Yamanashi Ring Road]] == References == {{Reflist|35em|qq=}} == Further Reading == * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Yamanashi Prefecture'' (Heibonsha, 1995) * Hiroshi Ishikawa, ''Compilation of Kai Kokushi'' (Yamanashi Regional Studies Association, 2014)
== See Also == * [[:en:Arakawa (disambiguation)|Arakawa (disambiguation)]] * {{ill|Forests of Water Sources|ja|水源の森百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Forests of Water Sources|1=Top 100 Water Resource Forests|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11548911}} {{River-stub|pref=Yamanashi|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|version=102260947|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:荒川 (山梨県)]]
